entry.title: Transform Your Industry with AI and SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects in Cycle 3-2024!
entry.content:
Ready to leverage AI and transform your industry? With SAP’s innovative technology, we can help you streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and improve efficiency.

Envision a future where data is processed instantly, revealing critical insights that drive your business forward. SAP’s AI capabilities make this possible with customized solutions designed for your sector – be it customer experience, supply chain, finance, or HR.

Here’s your chance to shape the future of AI. Join our SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, where your input will help us develop AI solutions that align with your unique needs. Your insights are vital for creating tools that foster your success.

Don’t wait—partner with us to harness AI for digital transformation and achieve outstanding results. Register for our SAP Customer Engagement Initiative today – don’t miss out; registration closes on November 15, 2024!

  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • AI-supported Extension of Electronic Document Processing of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Joule applications to SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data
  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Business AI
  • Research Project: Production Scheduling using AI
  • Research: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - AI: Field Mapping Proposal
  • Using AI to Extract Data from the Delivery Note to Update the Inbound Delivery

We believe that collaboration is key to unlocking innovation. By joining forces with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and change the way businesses operate using AI.

Seize this opportunity – register today on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3 in 2024! If you are also interested in other SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects that are currently open for registration, check out the full list here.

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Driving ABAP Innovation: Key highlights and future trends from SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
Dear Developer Community,

I am thrilled to share with you some of the inspiring ABAP highlights from SAP TechEd 2024. The sessions were packed with valuable information and insights that are sure to empower and motivate every ABAP developer out there.

If you haven't had a chance to watch the video SAP TechEd 2024 | ABAP Highlights, I highly recommend it as it provides a concise overview of the latest developments in ABAP and the incredible possibilities that lie ahead:

 

In this blog post, I would like to take the opportunity to acknowledge the incredible work of our talented speakers and reflect on the wealth of knowledge they shared.

ABAP Cloud in SAP Build

Alexander Rother’s, Björn Schulz’s and Andre Borchert’s session AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions emphasized the immense potential of the SAP ecosystem. The inclusion of ABAP Cloud in the SAP Build family marks a significant leap forward, forming the world’s strongest alliance for customer and partner development. By embracing the fusion development principle and harnessing the power of Generative AI, developers can expect unparalleled productivity and unprecedented outcomes. This transformative collaboration paves the way for innovation and advancement, driving the SAP ecosystem to new heights.

This integration was also discussed in session AD105 – Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build by Anne Keller and Tim Back. They showed a demo of how to integrate your ABAP Cloud projects into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

Clean Core Extensibility

The session DT200 | Clean Core extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition by Olga Dolinskaja and Thomas Fiedler provided essential guidance on transforming legacy development to ABAP Cloud and Clean Core by using classic SAP APIs for the 3-Tier Extensibility Model. It also captured the upcoming S/4HANA Joule Agent in ABAP Development Tools (ADT) and how it will boost the development efficiency and create exciting possibilities for the future.

The 3-Tier Extensibility Model and how it serves to transform classic ABAP custom code into Clean Core compliant custom, was also highlighted in session DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future by Carine Tchoutouo Djomo and Jan Gilg. By integrating of Generative AI across the entire SAP development lifecycle, they showcased the limitless possibilities for future innovation.

In session AD106 | Clean Core extensibility of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with ABAP Cloud, Volker Drees provided valuable insights into the Clean Core extensibility of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with ABAP Cloud. The overview of ABAP Cloud usage and the 3-Tier Extensibility Model, coupled with the integration of classic APIs (BAPIs) following the Clean Core principles, presented a roadmap for developers to create extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Andre Fischer's session DT280 | Build Clean Core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud demonstrated the power of the new generators in ADT for building Clean Core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The session showed how easy it is to use the Field Extension Wizard and the UI Services Wizard to create extensions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, empowering developers to unleash their creativity and take their applications to the next level.

Attendees of session DT201 | Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, presented by Alda Dollani and Thomas Schneider, gained valuable insights into finding the right extensibility tool for various use cases and identifying the right API or extension point. The session also showcased a cutting-edge extensibility wizard and AI capability, which is sure to revolutionize the way attendees approach their extensibility challenges.

ABAP AI

In the session AD107 | Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP developer capabilities, Jasmin Gruschke and Sebastian Baskovich focused on SAP's development of AI assistant tools for ABAP developers, with an exciting live demo and lab preview of Joule’s ABAP developer capabilities. ABAP AI truly is a game changer.

Carine Tchoutouo Djomo's sessions AD180 | Build extensions with ABAP Cloud and Joule's ABAP developer capabilities was an inspiring demonstration of the potential to accelerate application development using Joule ABAP developer capabilities. In her session, Carine demonstrated the implementation of business events with ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) for loosely coupled on-stack and side-by-side integration scenarios, as well as the display of hierarchical business data in SAP Fiori apps using Treeview.

In their session AI104 – Revolutionize ABAP development with Joule, our generative AI copilot Salma Sohrabi-Jahromi and Jens Weiler elaborated further on Joule's capabilities in ABAP development and how Joule can enrich everyday work.

Let me end this blog post with another video about the key TechEd Keynote messages about ABAP:

 

My heartfelt thanks to all the speakers and session owners for their invaluable contributions and insights.
All the best,

Sonja Liénard
SVP BTP ABAP

 

More to read

We recently published a blog post entitled The next step of ABAPs evolution: ABAP Cloud now part of SAP Build, which focuses on ABAP Cloud and its integration with SAP Build. I encourage you to read it if you haven't already.

On behalf of the entire ABAP community, I would also like to thank Michael Ameling for his continuous support and dedication to spreading the amazing ABAP news at TechEd 2024. His contributions have been invaluable, and we are incredibly grateful for his efforts. Check out his latest community blog post Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 24th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/10/24/sap-developer-news-october-24th-2024/

Description

SAP Build Apps now supports Actions!:

  • Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mE7nHx9XHAY&ab_channel=SAPDevelopers
  • Blog Post by Qui Shi: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-apps-integration-with-sap-ai-core-services-part-4-integration-via/ba-p/13885101
  • Official feature documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/actions

Devtoberfest Wrap Up

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd On Tour

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • ASUG Tech Connect 2024: https://events.asug.com/event/9a5f27ca-d742-45bb-85e7-a6597b952900/summary?RefId=sap-splash-page-home&utm_campaign=ATC_24&utm_medium=sap&utm_source=referral&utm_term=sap-splash-page-home
  • SAPinsider EMEA 2024: https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024?utm_source=marketing&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=teched&utm_content=sapmarketing
  • SAUG-x 2024: https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024
  • UKISUG Connect 2024: https://www.ukisugconnect.co.uk/techx-at-connect?utm_source=TechEd_Webpage&utm_medium=Website&utm_campaign=TECHx_at_Connect
  • DSAG-TechXchange 2025: https://dsag.de/neuigkeiten/sap-teched-goes-dsag-techxchange-english/

ABAPconf 2024

  • Event Site:  https://abapconf.org/abapconf2024/

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 SAP Build Apps now supports Actions!
1:26 Devtoberfest Wrap Up
3:45 TechEd On Tour
4:49 ABAPConf 2024

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for October 24th, 2024.

[Shrini] I hope you got a chance to watch this Devtoberfest session we did earlier this week on chatbot development made easy. If you haven't watched it, go ahead and watch the recording as I am using the same project to show you the newly added actions feature that is now available in SAP Build Apps. In the demo, I showed you how to create a data entity to connect to the Generative AI Hub and now with this feature, you could replace the data entity with an action project, configure the input, test, and save the output fields. Remember to add SAP Application Development Actions enabled to true to the destination in order for it to appear in the access project. After you release and publish the access to the library, you can enable it from the integrations tab. Now, there is also a trigger actions function available in the logic canvas and you could trigger this action while providing the BTP destination to get a response from the backend system. The response will now be available within outputs trigger action. Go ahead and try out the new actions feature in SAP Build Apps now, and you could replace a data entity in one of your build apps project with this new actions feature. You could refer to this blog post by Chu Shi, which has step by step instructions.

[Thomas] By the time you're seeing this new segment, our Devtoberfest for 2024 will have probably nearly come to a close. It's hard to imagine but this is the fifth year for Devtoberfest. I know it seems to us like just the other day we were planning our very first Devtoberfest and here we've grown to be this major part of your overall SAP TechEd experience. I'm really enjoying where we're at with Devtoberfest and TechEd. You know with TechEd you get the big announcements; you get introduction to new topics and then you can attend Devtoberfest session sessions to get a deeper dive into those same topics. It also allows us to cover a variety of maybe older topics, but ones that are still relevant to customers and partners out in the SAP ecosystem. So I'm really happy with the state where we are with Devtoberfest. It's great that we had over 60,000 unique people join and attend Devtoberfest sessions. We had tons of people, you know, almost 4,000 people enter the contest. We'll be announcing winners of the Devtoberfest contest soon. If all the validation of winners happens in enough time, we plan to announce those at the ASUG Tech Connect event, one of the SAP TechEd On Tour stops.

Speaking of SAP TechEd On Tour, that's really the next stage of continuing your TechEd journey. journey. If a Tekken on Tour stop is coming to an area near you, I encourage you to sign up and join to get that in-person experience with many of these new announced technologies. If it's not able to come your way, then we have lots of Code Jams planned for you next year, or if one's not coming to an area near you, consider hosting and request one to come to your area. We have plans to expand the parts of the world that we cover with Code Jams next year, hopefully making it to a few locations that we haven't been to in over four or five years. So with that, thank you, developer community, for another successful Devtoberfest, and we look forward to seeing you again in 2025.

[DJ] In this video I'm going to talk about innovating cutting-edge tech from the high seas. He could have kept his anchors away, but he didn't. Right. Tech out. I'll be coming up hot for a minute.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. I hope everyone is having fun with the last week of Devtoberfest. But today, I wanted to make sure that you all know about the ABAP conference, which will take place on December 5th, 2024. This is a fantastic community-driven event specifically for ABAP developers, which will be streamed online. But you can also enter a drawing for a ticket to one of the on-site venues. The content is delivered in both German and English, depending on the session, and there are a ton of familiar names on the agenda. Way too many to mention here, so head over to the abopconference.org website for more information and details around the agenda.

entry.title: Introducing the new Adaptation Editor experience for SAPUI5 adaptation projects
entry.content:
SAPUI5 flexibility to adapt standard SAP apps

SAPUI5 is SAP’s strategic Web framework for Web applications such as SAP Fiori. In this context, SAPUI5 flexibility offers capabilities to adapt standard apps, not only for developers but also for key users and end users using simple WYSIWYG drag and drop tools. Learn about all the capabilities: SAPUI5 Flexibility - All You Need to Know. SAPUI5 flexibility provides features to realize a clean core approach with on-stack extensions: SAP standard apps remain untouched, no modifications are needed, and even predefined extension points are not needed anymore.

Developers can leverage the concepts of SAPUI5 flexibility by using SAPUI5 adaptation projects in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), the development environment of SAP Build Code. Those projects contain all code-based custom adaptations, created in the various editors of BAS.

 

📣  Goodbye SAPUI5 Visual Editor, welcome Adaptation Editor

Following Introducing SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in Visual Studio Code I now have more news: To improve the development experience and harmonize the editors for SAPUI5 adaptation projects offered in BAS and VS Code, BAS now features the new Adaptation Editor, replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor.

Picture: New Adaptation Editor

The Adaptation Editor

  • … opens in a separate browser tab, offering more space for the editor and especially for the canvas showing the app preview. This let’s you view the (desktop) app in a more realistic size to understand how it will look like for your end users,
  • … launches and runs much faster and more robust. Keep in mind that for launching, it still needs to pull the standard app from your (on-prem) system,
  • … has a slightly changed UX flow with a dedicated button to explicitly save changes and then continue to work on your code in the BAS browser tab,
  • … adds support for SAP S/4HANA Cloud in VS Code,
  • … is now part of the “SAP Fiori Tools – ADP” extension of BAS, contained e.g. in dev spaces of type ‘SAP Fiori’. With this, the delivery of tools around SAP Fiori has a hamonized foundation for future capabilities, which will further enhance the developer experience.

The new Adaptation Editor

The Adaptation Editor provides an intuitive experience to work with adaptation projects. It features:

  • the Outline pane (top left) to understand the structure of controls on the page
  • the Changes (bottom left) you’ve done so far
  • the Canvas (middle) to preview your application and create changes
  • the Properties (right) pane view / edit control properties
  • A toolbar allowing you to use:
    • Navigation mode to ‘use’ the app and navigate through it until you found the area to adapt,
    • UI Adaptation mode to add custom changes. Selecting an element in the Canvas highlights it in the Outline and shows its properties,
    • Save to … well, save your changes after condensing them to the minumum.

Launch the new Adaptation Editor

See the SAP Help documentation on Adapting an Application. The high-level steps are:

  1. Create an SAPUI5 adaptation project in SAP Business Application Studio.
  2. In the Explorer, click Open Adaptation Editor in the context menu of your project. Alternatively, use the Command Palette to find the command Fiori: Open Adaptation Editor.

See more details in:

  • SAPUI5 Flexibility - All You Need to Know
  • SAP Business Application Studio: Extending SAP Fiori Applications
  • SAP Fiori Tools: Extending an Existing Application

entry.title: SAP Builder Day – Low-Code-Entwicklung mit SAP Build: Ein Event zur Zukunft des Upskillings
entry.content:
Am 12. November 2024 findet der virtuelle SAP Builder Day statt – eine großartige Gelegenheit, um die Möglichkeiten der Low-Code-Entwicklung mit SAP kennen zu lernen.

Highlights des Events

  • Virtuelles Event für alle Interessierten: Für Fachkräfte und Unternehmen, die sich in Low-Code-Entwicklung weiterbilden möchten, steht der SAP Builder Day offen.
  • Kennenlernen des SAP Build-Portfolios: Ein Überblick über die gesamte Produktpalette, darunter:
    • SAP Build Apps: App-Entwicklung ohne Code
    • Build Process Automation: Prozessautomatisierung leicht gemacht
    • Build Work Zone: Gestaltung einer eigenen Unternehmenswebsite
  • Hands-on-Ansatz: Direkter Praxiseinstieg durch interaktive Sessions:
    • Prozessautomatisierung live erstellen (ohne Programmierung)
    • Eigene App im Baukastenprinzip designen
    • Unternehmenswebsite gestalten und anpassen

 

Warum dieses Event wichtig ist

  • Steigende Bedeutung von Low-Code-Skills: Low-Code-Entwicklung wird für Unternehmen immer wichtiger, um flexibel und schnell auf Marktanforderungen zu reagieren.
  • Zugang für Nicht-IT-Experten: Praktische Einblicke und Fähigkeiten für Teilnehmer ohne tiefgehende Programmierkenntnisse.
  • Wertvolles Upskilling für die Arbeitswelt der Zukunft: Für Unternehmen und Fachkräfte, die ihre digitalen Kompetenzen ausbauen möchten, ist der SAP Builder Day die ideale Plattform.

 Der SAP Builder Day bietet also eine praxisorientierte und zugängliche Weiterbildung in Low-Code – und ist eine wertvolle Investition in die eigene digitale Zukunft.

 

Möchten Sie noch mehr Zugriff auf Lernressourcen on-demand? Entdecken Sie, was die SAP Learning-Seite für Sie bereithält:

entry.title: SAP Build - Actions
entry.content:
It’s been +2 years since I started using SAP Build Apps (formerly known as AppGyver) and I have seen the evolution of this tool. Just like any other tool, not perfect, but it has its good moments. In this blog, I would like to discuss the new feature called Actions.  To see this blog in Spanish, go here

 

 

Actions – in the context of SAP Build Apps – allow low code no code (LCNC) developers to encapsulate APIs in low code fashion and be able to use them from within the SAP build apps logic. All apps regardless of size do make backend calls in one way or another; think of SAP and non-SAP system integration.

In the past, I wrote about uploading files to AWS via lambda function and also consuming a REST API directly from the LCNC application (see Other blogs below).

In this blog, I would like to showcase, how to encapsulate the invocation of an AWS Lambda function call from the Actions point of view and then showcase how to use it from SAP Build Apps.

 

Assuming you already have set up the pre-reqs in your SAP Build Apps environment (official SAP blog here https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-trial..html

  • Enable Cloud identity services
  • Allow trust
  • Adding Role collections
  • Adding user to role collections

The previous steps will allow you to be able to access the SAP Build Apps tool to develop LCNC applications. Once your environment is set up…. You must create a Destination in the BTP cockpit and that you can access it from there. https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-cf-create-destination..html

* If your destination is created and accessible, then, you must add the following additional properties to make sure this is seen from the SAP Build Apps tool (https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-systems)

  1. To make your destination available from the SAP Build apps
  2. Allowing the destination to be used from the Actions section in SAP build apps

Property

value

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled

true

BuildApps.Enabled

true

BuildApps.Type

rest / odata

 

 

 

Actions are created from an Actions project as described in the official documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-actions-project

Now, there are different ways to achieve integration, such as, to consume BTP destinations, to consume cloud functions, to consume OData APIs from other SAP systems (direct integration to SuccessFactors, Concur, CAP, RAP). In my case, I am going to consume a REST API wrapped as an Action in my Build Apps application.

 

Once in SAP Build Apps, in the left nav, there is a Lobby, Connectors and Control Tower.

Lobby is where you can create your sap build apps (front and back end)

Connectors is where you can create Action projects based on API sources [RAP, CAP, BTP destinations, SuccessFactors, Concur)

 

When you open the Action project you created, you can see actions in your project.

When selected, each action has an endpoint, an Input section, an Output section and Test (self-explanatory)

 

 

The Test tab is my favorite here because you can verify that the destination is reachable, and that there is data flow into the SAP Build Apps tool (before doing this, you can also verify your service from POSTMAN or even that the connection to the Destinations works in your BTP cockpit)

 

Once in the tab, you can select the Manual (you will type a URL, authentication type or you can select a destination if you set up the additional properties in your BTP cockpit Destination section as shown above *

Further, there is a section to provide your input values in case you must pass input parameters (query string, headers, path). If all is set up correctly, and you get a successful response (after clicking on the Test button) then, you will be able to analyze the response by clicking on the View API link on bottom right of the screen (it is kind of hidden, but it is there).

A Test Preview section opens and you can see the response from your Action. If you get here and you can see a response, congratulations. This is a good spot to be in. Now, you don’t need to know the exact schema of your output. Once you have a successful response, you can assign it to be pre-populated by clicking on Generate Output button (This feature is a 5 star in my opinion). Now you can consume data, pass params, and get a response all from SAP Build Apps.

 

 

If you make a new Action or make changes to an existing action, please make sure to click on the Release button and then Publish it to be reflected in the SAP Build Apps side.

 

The next step and new feature is the logic flow within the SAP Build Apps itself. I will not go into detail on how to create an app since I have mentioned several blogs below that you can reference to get you started, or even better, jump over the Builders group in #SAPCommunity and see what is going on in that community group for more examples and to connect with other SAP Builders like me.

In the image below, I opened the SAP Build apps and I had already added some components to the canvas. I knew I wanted to invoke the AWS Lambda function from the click of a button after reading an input from a user.

  • The input field sets the value to a Page Variable
  • The button click event triggers the flow below
    1. If condition to verify there is a non-empty value, otherwise show an Alert
    2. The Trigger action calls the Action which moreover calls the destination and therefore the lambda function 😊
    3. Upon response from the Action, I am showing a Toast message with the (JSON) response

 

 


Additional new logic flows exist there that I am not mentioning in this blog post. I encourage you to investigate those. I wanted to see how this new feature works and see the easiness (or difficulty level) to accomplish this integration between SAP Build Apps, BTP - Destinations, and AWS Lambda).

 

 

As I conclude this blog, I truly believe this exercise was easier than I thought.

  • It saved me time due to the LCNC approach.
  • It saved me during the integration process so that I didn’t have to know how to structure that in a pro-coding environment.
  • It saved me the headache to create types/classes by leveraging the Generate Output structure.
  • It saved me time to validate the integration and making sure the data was flowing

 

Things that didn’t make sense to me:

SAP Logo on top right – is this the only way to go back to the home page (Lobby, Connectors)

Actions à versions – how many can we keep? Why should we need many versions?)

Publishing an action should automatically update the version in SAP Build apps, however, when I navigated to the Integrations section of the app itself, I had to click on the bubble showing me it has now been upgraded.

 

Gotcha:

When you download the SAP Build apps preview apps, make sure to select the correct region prior to generating a pin, otherwise you will spin your wheels getting incorrect ping message.

 

Other blogs I did in the past;

  • To code or not to code, that is the question: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/appgyver-to-code-or-not-to-code-that-is-the-question/ba-p/13544885
  • Master list detail - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/appgyver-master-detail-scenario/ba-p/13526977
  • Uploading a file to AWS (via calling lambda function) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/build-bros-handling-file-upload-via-http-request/ba-p/13551643
  • Funny app for kids - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/dadiambored-no-code-challenge/ba-p/13530701

 

Here is the same app from my mobile device

After calling the Action  --> 

 

 

 

Thank you for your time reading this blog. Please let me know if you have any questions, comments or suggestions.

 

Happy Low/No Coding!

SG

entry.title: SAP Build - Actions (Spanish)
entry.content:
Han pasado más de dos años desde que comencé a usar SAP Build Apps (anteriormente conocido como AppGyver) y he visto la evolución de esta herramienta. Como cualquier otra herramienta, no es perfecta, pero tiene sus buenos momentos. En este blog, me gustaría hablar sobre la nueva función llamada Actions. Para ver este blog en ingles, haga clic aquí.

 

Actions (acciones) en el contexto de SAP Build Apps permiten a los desarrolladores de bajo código/no código (LCNC) encapsular APIs de forma sencilla y usarlas dentro de la lógica de SAP Build Apps. Todas las aplicaciones, independientemente de su tamaño, realizan llamadas a backend de una forma u otra; piense en la integración de sistemas SAP y no SAP.

Anteriormente, escribí sobre la carga de archivos a AWS mediante una función lambda y también sobre el consumo de una API REST directamente desde una aplicación LCNC (ver otros blogs al final del blog).

En este blog, quiero mostrar cómo encapsular la invocación de una llamada a la función AWS Lambda desde el punto de vista de las acciones y luego mostrar cómo usarla desde SAP Build Apps.

 

Asumiendo que ya ha configurado los requisitos previos en su entorno de SAP Build Apps (blog oficial de SAP aquí https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-trial..html😞

  1. Habilitar los servicios de identidad en la nube (Cloud identity services)
  2. Permitir la confianza (allow trust)
  3. Agregar colecciones de roles (role collections)
  4. Agregar usuarios a colecciones de roles

Los pasos anteriores le permitirán acceder a la herramienta SAP Build Apps para desarrollar aplicaciones LCNC. Una vez que su entorno esté configurado... debe crear un Destino en el cockpit de BTP y acceder a él desde allí. https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-cf-create-destination..html

  • Si su destino está creado y accesible, debe agregar las siguientes propiedades adicionales para asegurarse de que sea visible desde la herramienta SAP Build Apps https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-systems:
  • Hacer disponible el destino desde SAP Build Apps
  • Permitir el uso del destino desde la sección "Actions" en SAP Build Apps Propiedad Valor

Property

value

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled

true

BuildApps.Enabled

true

BuildApps.Type

rest / odata

 

 

    •  

Actions se crean desde un proyecto de acciones, como se describe en la documentación oficial: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-actions-project.

Ahora, existen diferentes formas de lograr la integración, tales como, consumir destinos de BTP, consumir funciones en la nube o consumir APIs OData de otros sistemas SAP (integración directa con SuccessFactors, Concur, CAP, RAP). En mi caso, voy a consumir una API REST envuelta como una Acción en mi aplicación de Build Apps.

Una vez en SAP Build Apps, en la navegación de la izquierda, hay un Lobby, Connectors y Control Tower.

  • Lobby es donde puede crear sus aplicaciones SAP Build Apps (front y back end).
  • Connectors es donde puede crear proyectos de acciones basados en fuentes de API [RAP, CAP, destinos BTP, SuccessFactors, Concur].

 

Cuando abre el proyecto de Acción que creó, puede ver las acciones en su proyecto. Al seleccionarla, cada acción tiene un endpoint, una sección de Input, una sección de Output y una Test (autoexplicativo).

 

La pestaña Test es mi favorita porque puede verificar que el destino sea alcanzable y que haya flujo de datos en la herramienta SAP Build Apps (antes de hacer esto, también puede verificar su servicio desde POSTMAN o incluso que la conexión con los Destinos funcione en su cockpit de BTP).

Una vez en la pestaña, puede seleccionar la opción Manual (escribirá una URL, tipo de autenticación o puede seleccionar un destino si configuró las propiedades adicionales en su sección de Destinos del cockpit de BTP como se mostró arriba). Además, hay una sección para proporcionar sus valores de entrada en caso de que deba pasar parámetros de entrada (query string, header, path). Si todo está configurado correctamente y obtiene una respuesta exitosa (después de hacer clic en el botón de Test), podrá analizar la respuesta haciendo clic en el enlace "Ver API" en la parte inferior derecha de la pantalla (está un poco oculto, pero está allí).

Se abrirá una sección de Vista previa de prueba y podrá ver la respuesta de su Acción. Si llega aquí y puede ver una respuesta, ¡felicitaciones! Está en un buen lugar. Ahora no necesita conocer el esquema exacto de su salida. Una vez que tenga una respuesta exitosa, puede asignarla para que se pre-poblen los datos haciendo clic en el botón "Generar Output" (esta característica merece 5 estrellas en mi opinión). Ahora puede consumir datos, pasar parámetros y obtener una respuesta, todo desde SAP Build Apps.

 

Si crea una nueva Acción o realiza cambios en una acción existente, asegúrese de hacer clic en el botón "Release" y luego hacer Publish para que se refleje en SAP Build Apps.

El siguiente paso y nueva característica es el flujo de lógica dentro de SAP Build Apps en sí. No entraré en detalles sobre cómo crear una aplicación, ya que mencioné varios blogs a continuación que puede consultar para comenzar, o mejor aún, visite el grupo Builders en #SAPCommunity para ver qué sucede en ese grupo y conectarse con otros Constructores SAP como yo.

En la imagen a continuación, abrí SAP Build Apps y ya había agregado algunos componentes al lienzo. Sabía que quería invocar la función AWS Lambda desde el clic de un botón después de leer una entrada de un usuario.

  1. El campo de Input establece el valor en una Variable de Página
  2. El evento de clic del botón activa el flujo a continuación:
  1. Condición para verificar que hay un valor no vacío; de lo contrario, mostrar una Alerta.
  2. La Trigger Action llama a la Acción que además llama al destino y, por lo tanto, a la función Lambda 😊
  3. Tras la respuesta de la Acción, muestro un mensaje de Toast con la respuesta (JSON).

 

 

Existen otros nuevos flujos de lógica que no menciono en este blog. Los invito a investigarlos. Quería ver cómo funcionaba esta nueva característica y la facilidad (o dificultad) para lograr esta integración entre SAP Build Apps, BTP - Destinations y AWS Lambda.

Al concluir este blog, realmente creo que este ejercicio fue más fácil de lo que pensé.

  1. Me ahorró tiempo debido al enfoque LCNC.
  2. Me ahorró en el proceso de integración, evitando tener que saber cómo estructurarlo en un entorno de codificación profesional.
  3. Me ahorró el dolor de cabeza de crear types/clases aprovechando la estructura de Generar Output.
  4. Me ahorró tiempo al validar la integración y asegurarme de que los datos fluyeran.

Cosas que no tuvieron sentido para mí:

  • Logo de SAP en la esquina superior derecha: ¿es esta la única forma de volver a la página de inicio (Lobby, Connectors)?
  • Acciones → versiones: ¿cuántas podemos mantener? ¿Por qué necesitaríamos muchas versiones?
  • Publicar una acción debería actualizar automáticamente la versión en SAP Build Apps; sin embargo, cuando navegué a la sección de Integraciones de la aplicación en sí, tuve que hacer clic en la burbuja que me mostraba que se había actualizado.

Sugerencia: Cuando descargue las aplicaciones de vista previa de SAP Build Apps, asegúrese de seleccionar la región correcta antes de generar un PIN; de lo contrario, obtendrá mensajes de error de pin incorrectos.

Otros blogs que escribí en el anteriormente:

  1. "To code or not to code, that is the question": https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/appgyver-to-code-or-not-to-code-that-is-the-question/ba-p/13544885
  2. "Master list detail" - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/appgyver-master-detail-scenario/ba-p/13526977
  3. "Uploading a file to AWS (via calling lambda function)" - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/build-bros-handling-file-upload-via-http-request/ba-p/13551643
  4. "Funny app for kids" - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/dadiambored-no-code-challenge/ba-p/13530701

Aquí está la misma aplicación desde mi dispositivo móvil.

 

  Después de llamar la acción à  

 

Gracias por su tiempo leyendo este blog. Por favor, hágame saber si tiene alguna pregunta, comentario o sugerencia.

¡Feliz desarrollo de bajo/no código!

SG

entry.title: SAP is a Visionary in 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms
entry.content:
Today, I am very proud to share the great news: Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP).

Let me share with you how we got here, what we believe makes us a Visionary in low-code application development with SAP Build and how you can get started with SAP’s one-stop-shop for extensibility and automations.

SAP Build – the low-code platform for SAP customers

We have designed SAP Build as the best software suite for SAP customers that want to efficiently extend their SAP solutions: SAP Build fosters fusion development allowing active collaboration between business users and professional developers, it brings Business AI with Joule copilot to boost development productivity, prebuilt LoB solutions with context-based recommendations, and deep integration with business applications.

A bit of history

Two years ago, at SAP TechEd 2022, we announced the launch of a powerful new offering to drive the next wave of business transformation by unleashing the expertise of those who know best in the lines of business.
SAP Build has been designed to put SAP’s world-class enterprise technology in the hands of multidisciplinary teams in business and IT, giving them direct, secure access to the end-to-end processes, data and context they need to make smarter decisions and drive innovation quickly. SAP Build allows business experts and, in the SAP world “Key Users”, to fruitfully collaborate with IT and developers to iterate and co-create new innovations.

Last year, at SAP TechEd 2023, we announced SAP Build Code, which went generally available in March this year. Tailored for SAP development, SAP Build Code is a comprehensive, turn-key solution for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. Developers can leverage Joule to efficiently generate application logic, data models, and test scripts. SAP Build Code enhances the entire development process, from design time to runtime, significantly boosting developers’ productivity.

And recently at SAP TechEd 2024 we achieved another milestone by adding ABAP Environments to SAP Build: with this, SAP Build is THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development.

SAP Build is now the fastest growing area in SAP Business Technology Platform: we have doubled our revenue over the last year, increasing by more than 70% the active customer base. And we have had more than 200,000 active learners.

What makes SAP a visionary with SAP Build?

From my personal point of view, here are the key factors that make SAP Build the best development platform for SAP customers:

Fusion Team Development with one SAP Build offering

SAP is promoting the concept of fusion team development. Instead of converting pro-developers s to low-code developers (or vice versa), it combines its traditional developer stack with low-code SAP Build products. This multipronged development strategy resonates with enterprise developers and IT leaders who want the flexibility to use a range of approaches to build custom applications.

Fusion development is an enabler to be quicker with extending applications to your needs. What’s more, it is the foundation of allowing multidisciplinary teams to create connected solutions that fit the business’ needs, adds more people to the development and innovation equation which allows a much faster time to market, more fine-grained solutions shaped to the experts’ needs, and brings diversity into the innovation process.

Among our key innovations in Fusion Development:

  • Easy Project Discovery: Find, share and reuse application components with a centralized project lobby
  • Seamless Teamwork: Multiple developers can work simultaneously with version control and synchronized changes
  • Low-Code with Pro-Code Power: Business users can easily integrate pre-built solutions created by developers
  • Intuitive Iteration: Modify application, process & workspace designs and logic with drag-and drop ease, fostering collaboration between business and IT

Integrated Business AI capabilities

Assistance is what we expect from AI, to automate tedious tasks or to become faster with our assignments. Leveraging AI to speed up development productivity includes content recommendation sensitive to a certain context you find yourself in, allows you to naturally describe a process you’d like to create or even a data model to be created so you can focus on the creative parts of the app dev cycle, for instance, the UI creation. For that, SAP has integrated in SAP Build advanced AI capabilities that are grounded in business data: SAP Business AI understands business lingo and context. In addition, we recently launched at SAP TechEd the Joule Studio in SAP Build that will help customers and partners to build extensibility scenarios for Joule with custom skills and AI agents. So, Generative AI and Joule copilot are infused everywhere in SAP Build:

  • Application Development: Creation of business applications and automations
  • Process Automation: Process generation, process monitoring, document extraction and pre-built solution recommendations
  • Integration: Integration flow generation, pre-built integration recommendations
  • Joule Studio in SAP Build: Creating custom AI agents and skills for Joule copilot

Prebuilt LoB solutions

Content that allows developers to start with a ready-made template for their development is key for speeding up the creation process. Therefore, we are and have been investing heavily into content creation, be it ready-made APIs, process content packages, or application templates. Anything that is needed from a developer’s perspective to ease their work and make them faster in their creation process is available at hand in SAP Build available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Both SAP and partners contribute, maintain and update pre-built content. We have now more than 500 content packages with 120+ in Finance, 115+ in Supply Chain, 40+ in Human Resources and our recent innovations in this area include, but are not limited to:

  • Finance: Capital expenditure application, automation of accrual posting and more
  • Supply Chain: Process approval for SAP IBP, Purchase requisition approval for Ariba and more
  • Human Resources: Translate job postings, position management action and more

Deep integration with business apps

SAP is unifying and standardizing its application runtimes and developer tools for numerous developer personas and use cases. This new product composition makes it easier for customers to consume SAP Build and establish partnership links. It also provides the SAP Store and SAP Business Accelerator Hub offering prebuilt reusable components that accelerate application delivery.

Recently, we have delivered:

  • Navigation from the business application UI with contextualized Extensibility Wizard to initiate an SAP Build project: available already in in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, and soon in other SAP applications, the Extensibility Wizard is an embedded experience allowing key users to extend business applications – both on stack and side by side on SAP BTP – directly out of their business application without losing context.

For more details on how to set up the Extensibility Wizard and what extensions options are available, please read this blog post: “SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” and watch this Devtoberfest session by @Sesh_Sreenivas and @Karthik-Thiru.

  • Extensive library of connectors, integration flows and business events for SAP and non-SAP applications in SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

Learn more and get started with SAP Build!

To learn how SAP Build can optimize your finance, procurement, human resources, and more take the next step with the following resources:

  • Visit the SAP Build product page
  • If you are using S/4HANA, visit also our SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA page
  • Discover pre-built content packages on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Explore the basic trial and start your learning journey
  • Activate your entitlements if you’re an existing RISE and GROW with SAP customer

Use SAP Build now, not only to easily create applications, but also to automate processes and to design digital workspaces with low code and generative AI!

 

 

GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner and Magic Quadrant is a registered trademark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and are used herein with permission. All rights reserved.

This graphic was published by Gartner, Inc. as part of a larger research document and should be evaluated in the context of the entire document. The Gartner document is available upon request from SAP.

Gartner does not endorse any vendor, product or service depicted in its research publications, and does not advise technology users to select only those vendors with the highest ratings or other designation. Gartner research publications consist of the opinions of Gartner’s research organization and should not be construed as statements of fact. Gartner disclaims all warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this research, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.

entry.title: Steer the future direction of SAP Build at the SAP BTP Usability Event
entry.content:
Do you work with SAP Build products like Workzone, CDS-Modeller, Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, UI5? Would you like to help steer the future direction of these products?
Then our exclusive 2-day SAP BTP Usability Event is for you!
(Last remaining seats still available. Book yours by November 15th)

Join us on November 25th & 26th, 2024 at our headquarters in Walldorf, where you can:

  • Gain early insights into SAP's product vision, ideas, and research projects
  • Share ideas directly with the development teams and influence product development
  • Participate in the development of products that better meet your needs and help your business
  • Provide valuable input on new features directly to the engineering teams during 1:1 usability testing sessions
  • Access expert talks and networking opportunities

What next? Please write me a DM (direct message) before November 15th to secure your place, and I can send you all the details around the registration, full agenda, accommodation etc.

Looking forward to seeing you there! #btpusabilityevent #sapbuild #usertesting

entry.title: Discover TechEd “Live” at SAPinsider EMEA 2024
entry.content:
Following hot on the heels of the hugely successful SAP TechEd Virtual event last month, attention now turns to a number of live events that will feature TechEd content and speakers, starting at the ASUG Tech Connect event in West Palm Beach, the first of SAP’s “TechEd on Tour” locations, as well as featuring in sessions at the SAPinsider EMEA “TechInsider” event in Copenhagen. Both events run from 12- 14 November in these wonderful, if quite different, locations.

I’ve been deeply involved in the planning for the SAPinsider event again, and for this reason alone will focus my blog on that event, though I am hopeful that both events will deliver a rich experience of TechEd content for attendees, and allow them to delve deeply into SAP technologies, particularly relating to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

At SAPinsider Copenhagen we’re going the extra mile, in trying to deliver a TechEd-style experience for attendees, who I’m happy to say will not only find an agenda littered with TechEd sessions, delivered by many of our stellar TechEd speakers, but also they’ll be able to participate in multiple hands-on sessions as well as having an opportunity to continue their learning experience by networking with SAP’s Developer Advocates who will guide and support attendees as they work through a number of the tutorials available at the Developer Garage on the show floor – which we are pleased will make its very first appearance at this SAPinsider event.

With all this on offer I’m excited for what’s in store in Copenhagen next week – and I hope that you are too!

Plan Ahead – And Set Your Agenda Now!

For those of you registered for the event, and in a manner which is not unlike visiting a Disney theme park, my suggestion for you to get the most out of an SAPinsider event is to plan ahead – not so much to beat the queues as you might at the theme park, but more so to make sure that you see all you want to see, and achieve what you set out to do. And the best way to do this is to prepare your agenda for the event before you go.

The first step is of course to get the event app, “SAPinsider Events”, available on the App Store and Google Play, and once you’ve installed that you’re good to go in terms of adding sessions to your personal agenda. Now for the fun bit – what sessions to see? Perhaps I might help with some ideas.

I’ll assume that everyone will plan to join the day 1 and day 2 keynotes. I’m looking forward to both, the motivational first day keynote from Hein Wagner, starting at 9.00am on Tuesday 12th November, followed the next day by the AI inspired second day keynote, featuring Martin Böhm of Ottobock and Jonathan Von Rüden of SAP, and which starts at the same time of day on the 13th November.

But after the keynotes, what next? Here are my personal 6 TechEd inspired picks for Copenhagen:

  1. Enhancing SAP Development Processes with SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Interoperability to Elevate Your Extensions, 12-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  2. Hands-On Lab: Develop Applications and Processes with SAP Build and Joule, 12-Nov-24, 15:30 - 17:30
  3. Mastering SAP Business AI for Enhanced Performance and Innovation, 13-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  4. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere Solutions: Integration, 13-Nov-24, 14:15 - 15:00
  5. Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 13-Nov-24, 16:45 - 17:30
  6. SAP BTP Strategy and Highlights, 14-Nov-24, 09:00 - 09:45

Of course, this is just the tip of the iceberg, as there are many more sessions available for you to select from the agenda, and among which you’ll find more TechEd content, as well as content from other speakers from SAP and our business partners, in addition to many customer case stories too. And in a shameless plug too, you may also note my session “Navigating Your Path to Success with SAP BTP” which I shall deliver alongside Nis Boy Naeve on 13 November at 11:30am. Please join us if you can! Take a look at the SAPinsider event website sessions list, or the event app that you’ve already downloaded to see more.

Your Continued Learning Experience

If you’d like to continue your own personal learning journey after the event, then why not explore other learning opportunities from SAP? For example, learn how to build software applications, side-by-side extensions, and integrations to and from cloud applications by exploring SAP’s free learning content on SAP BTP. It is made for both integration designers and extension developers from all levels of expertise and will help you stay up to date with the latest SAP BTP innovations. And check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on SAP Learning site.

Or if you’re interested in TechEd sessions, but can’t join the events in West Palm Beach or Copenhagen, then perhaps another TechEd on Tour location, in Australia, Germany or the UK would work better for you?

For those of you travelling to Copenhagen next week, I do hope that you find SAPinsider EMEA 2024 to be a truly informative and stimulating experience. And, of course, if you visit the show floor then look me up at the SAP exhibit booth, and I’ll be happy to have a chat with you about SAP BTP, the conference or just about the amazing city of Copenhagen!

My best wishes for safe travels, and I hope that you enjoy the event!

Chris Grundy

Product Marketing, SAP BTP
